english: virtuous actions, mi-yyo-bai las-rgyu-°brás (mi g.yo ba'i las rgyu 'bras) ascetic or mystical works W.), or for lás-kyi rgyu-°brás: (las kyi rgyu 'bras:) fruits of works, retribution and the doctrine of it. -- lás-sgo (las sgo) trading-place, emporium Glr. -- lás-ċan (las can) 1. laborious, industrious Cs. 2. (v. above las 3) having acquired merit, worthy Mil. -- las-čé (las che) in C. used for expressing probability, as in W. °gro ('gro) with the gerund is used, v. °gro-ba ('gro ba) I, 5; mtoṅ las-čé (mthong las che) he will probably have seen it Mil. nt.; ṅas °di °bor las-čé (ngas 'di 'bor las che) as possibly I may put this yet aside; kyod mi-la-ni min las-čé (khyod mi la ni min las che) you are not Mila, are you? Mil. -- las tog-pa (las thog pa) Sch.: a person employed, an official, a functionary. -- las-rtágs (las rtags) Sch. dignity, rank, title incident to the office held. -- las-dáṅ-po-pa (las dang po pa) v. daṅ-po (dang po). -- las-dár (las dar) Sch.: 'parade, ceremonial'(?) -- lás-pa (las pa) 1. workman, labourer Cs. 2. Sp.: vice-magistrate of a village. -- lás-dpon (las dpon) overseer of workmen. -- las-spyód (las spyod) works, actions, way of life, byaṅ-čúb-kyi las-spyód (byang chub kyi las spyod) skyéd-pa (bskyed pa) to lead a holy life Pth. -- las-°pró (las 'phro) 'continuation, prosecution of works', blessings following meritorious deeds, kyed daṅ ṅa yaṅ sṅón-gyi las-°pró-yód-pa yin (khyed dang nga yang sngon gyi las 'phro yod pa yin) a bond of connection is formed between you and me by the merits we acquired in former periods of life Pth.; -- happiness, prosperity in consequence of good works, good luck, fortunate event, opp. to lan-čags (lan chags). -- las-°brél (las 'brel) Glr. prob. id. -- lás-mi (las mi) workman. -- las-méd (las med) idle, lazy, inactive. -- las-tsán (las tshan) 1. office, post, service, las-tsán-du °júg-pa (las tshan du 'jug pa) to put into office, to appoint, las-tsán-nas °dón-pa (las tshan nas 'don pa) to put out of office, to dismiss Cs. 2. official, functionary *yúl-gyi lẹ-tsẹ́n* (yul gyi las tshan) elders of a village-community C., las-tsán-pa (las tshan pa) id. -- *lẹ-lam-k'an* (las lam mkhan) diligent, industrious, *lẹ-mi-lám-k'an* (las mi lam mkhan) idle, lazy W. -- las-su byá-ba (las su bya ba) the second case of Tibetan grammar, the dative case. II. only in B. and C.: postp. c. accus. mostly corresponding in its application to the English prepos. from, used also for expressing the ablative case (having nearly the same sense as nas): 1. from, e.g. delivering from, coming from, often = through, e.g. shining into a room through the window Dzl.; to hear, get, borrow a thing from a person etc.; to call, to denominate a thing from or after, according to; tsád-las dpág-pa (tshad las dpag pa) to define by or according to measure S.g.; in quotations: °dúl-ba-las ('dul ba las) out of the, from the Dulva, sometimes also for: in the Dulva; for denoting the material of which a thing is made: of earth, of clay etc.; partitively: °bras dé-las šas yčíg ('bras de las shas gcig) a part of this rice, slób-ma-las yčíg (slob ma las gcig) one of the disciples Dzl.; ṅai yúl-mi-las bú-moyód-pa-rnams (ngai yul mi las bu mo yod pa rnams) the girls that are found among my subjects Dzl., kún-las °págs-pa (kun las 'phags pa) distinguished amongst all, more excellent than all the others Dzl.; hence 2. than after the comparative degree: ṅá-niṅ-las bzaṅ (nga ning las bzang) more beautiful than last year Mil.; with a negative: lo bču-drúg-las ma lón-te (lo bcu drug las ma lon te) not older than sixteen years Dzl.; zlá-ba lṅá-las mi sdod (zla ba lnga las mi sdod) I shall not stay longer than five months Glr.; ras-yúg yčíg-las mi bdóg-ste (ras yug gcig las mi bdog ste) possessing nothing but one sheet of cotton cloth Dzl.; ṅá-las med (nga las med) there is none besides myself Glr.; brnyas °kyér-ba-las mi yoṅ (brnyas 'khyer ba las mi yong) in the end you will probably do nothing else but despise me Mil.; in a brief mode of speaking: gsa-yčíg-las rje-btsun ma mtoṅ (gsa' gcig las rje btsun ma mthong) we saw nothing but the leopard, your Reverence we did not see Mil.; mi páṅ-žiṅ gnód-pa-las med (mi phang zhing gnod pa las med) it is good for nothing, it only does harm Mil. -- 3. added to the inf. of verbs it signifies not so much from as after, from doing, i.e. after doing, nyál-ba-las láṅ-ba (nyal ba las lang ba) to rise from lying, to rise after having been lying down; during, frq., the verbal root being repeated, soṅ-sóṅ-ba-las (song song ba las) during my going or travelling on Dzl.; ṅáṅ-du °gró-bar bsám-pa-las (ngang du 'gro bar bsam pa las) when (I) intended to walk in, when (I) was on the point of walking in Dzl.
Cs. for lús-pa (lus pa); in rág-las-pa (rag las pa) and a few other expressions occurring also in B.

I. bell-metal, li-sku (li sku), li-tál (li thal), li-túr (li thur), li-snód (li snod) an idol, a plate, spoon, vessel made of that metal; lí-ma (li ma) in gen.: utensil, instrument that is cast of li (li) Glr. II. apple, = sli (sli) C. III. li-yul (li yul) Glr., acc. to Was. (74) Bud-<
